english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Tom counts and categorizes birds that land in the tree in his backyard every evening from 10:00-11:00 pm and from 2:00-3:00 am. He counts mostly owls and generalizes, “most birds that land in my tree throughout the night are owls.”
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. the man counts and puts birds that land in the tree in his yard every night from the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down to the time when the sun goes down

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

A poll administered to 10,000 registered voters who were home-owners showed that 90% supported a policy to slash Medicaid funding and decrease property taxes. Therefore, 90% of voters support a policy to slash Medicaid funding.
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. a group of questions that was given to ten hundred people who were people who own homes and who were people who have homes showed that 9 out of ten said yes to a plan to cut money for the help that helps people who have kids and make the money that the people who run the land ask for less. so 9 out of ten of the people who live there say yes to a plan to cut money for the help that helps people who have kids.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

A telephone poll administered by a computer randomly generating numbers to call, found that 68% of Americans in the sample of 2000 were in favor of legalizing recreational marijuana use. Thus, almost 70% of Americans favor legalizing recreation marijuana use.
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. a call ask asked by a computer that makes numbers by chance to call, found that about six out of ten of the people from the country in the group that was ten years ago said they should stop using the green thing that people use for fun. so about seven out of ten of the people from the country said they should stop using the green thing that people use for fun.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

A randomized telephone poll in the United States asked respondents whether they supported a) a policy that allows killing innocent children in the womb or b) a policy that saves the lives of innocent children in the womb. The results showed that 69% of respondents choose option “b” over option “a.” The generalization was made that “most Americans favor a policy that disallows abortion.”
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the number big idea has. a call ask in the big country in the country asked the people if they liked the first thing a plan that lets kill children who are not bad or the second thing a plan that saves the lives of children who are not bad. the numbers showed that most of the people pick the second thing instead of the first thing. the big idea was made that most of the people in the country like a plan that stops the end of a baby.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Steve’s first rock and roll concert was an Ani Difranco concert, in which most of the concert-goers were women with feminist political slogans written on their t-shirts. Steve makes the generalization that “most rock and roll concert-goers are women who are feminists.” He then applies this generalization to the next concert he attends (Tom Petty) and is greatly surprised by what he finds.
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about numbers has. the first rock and roll show was a music show, and most of the people who go to the show were women with big ideas about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women who have a say about women

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

A high school principal conducts a survey of how satisfied students are with his high school by asking students in detention to fill out a satisfaction survey. Generalizing from that sample, he infers that 79% of students are dissatisfied with their high school experience. He is surprised and saddened by the result. 147
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. a high school head does a set of questions about how happy students are with his high school by asking students to put in a set of questions about happy students. saying that from that set he thinks that about seven out of ten of the students are not happy with their high school work. he feels sad and feels sad because of the answer.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

After having attended numerous Pistons home games over 20 years, Alice cannot remember a time when she didn’t see ticket scalpers selling tickets outside the stadium. She generalizes that there are always scalpers at every Pistons home game.
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. after she has gone to many games for the team that plays in a city in the east over twenty years, the person cannot remember a time when she did not see people who sell paper that lets you go out of the big place where people watch games. she says that there are always people who sell paper that lets you go out at every game for the team that plays in a city in the east.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

After having attended numerous Pistons home games over 20 years, Alice cannot remember a time when she didn’t see ticket scalpers selling tickets outside the stadium. She generalizes that there are ticket scalpers at every NBA game.
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. after she has gone to many home games for the team that has a name that starts with the first letter of the word group over twenty years, the first person cannot remember a time when she did not see people who sell paper that lets you go out of the big place where people watch games. she says that there are people who sell paper that lets you go out at every big game for the team that has a name that starts with the letter group.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

After having attended numerous Pistons home games over 20 years, Alice cannot remember a time when she didn’t see ticket scalpers selling tickets outside the stadium. She generalizes that there are ticket scalpers at every sporting event.
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. after she has gone to many games for the team that plays a ball game for twenty years, the first person cannot remember a time when she did not see people who sell paper that lets you go out of the big place where people watch games. she says that there are people who sell paper that lets you go at every game.

english_source:
Identify what kind of problem, if any, the statistical generalization has.

Bob once ordered a hamburger from Burger King and got violently ill shortly after he ate it. From now on, he never eats at Burger King because he fears he will get food poisoning.
ugf_translation: find what kind of problem, if any, the big idea about many things has. the man once ordered a hot food from the big place that makes food and got very sick soon after he ate it. from now on, he never eats at the big place that makes food because he is afraid he will get a bad thing in food.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Every painting by Rembrandt contains dark colors and illuminated faces, therefore the original painting that hangs in my high school is probably by Rembrandt, since it contains dark colors and illuminated faces.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a picture as strong or not strong. every picture made by the first person has dark colors and bright faces, so the first picture that hangs in my high school is probably made by the first person, because it has dark colors and bright faces.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

I was once bitten by a poodle. Therefore, this poodle will probably bite me too.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a small animal as strong or not strong. i was once hurt by a small animal. so this small animal will probably hurt me too.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Every poodle I’ve ever met has bitten me (and I’ve met over 300 poodles). Therefore, this poodle will probably bite me too.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a small animal as strong or not strong. every small animal that eats small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that eat small animals that

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

My friend took Dr. Van Cleave’s logic class last semester and got an A. Since Dr. Van Cleave’s class is essentially the same this semester and since my friend is no better a student than I am, I will probably get an A as well.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a picture as strong or not strong. my friend took the class of the person who works with thinking last time and got a good mark. because the class of the person who works with thinking is really the same this time and because my friend is not more good at a student than i am, i will probably get a good mark as well.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Bill Cosby used his power and position to seduce and rape women. Therefore, Bill Cosby probably also used his power to rob banks.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a story as strong or not strong. the first person used his power and place to make up and kill women. so the first person probably also used his power to take money from banks.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Every car I’ve ever owned had seats, wheels and brakes and was also safe to drive. This used car that I am contemplating buying has seats, wheels and brakes. Therefore, this used car is probably safe to drive.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a thing as strong or not strong. every car i have ever owned had seats, wheels and wheels and was also safe to drive. this used car that i am thinking about buying has seats, wheels and wheels. so this used car is probably safe to drive.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Every Volvo I’ve ever owned was a safe car to drive. My new car is a Volvo. Therefore, my new car is probably safe to drive.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a thing as strong or not strong. every big car i have ever owned was a safe car to drive. my new car is a big car. so my new car is probably safe to drive.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Dr. Van Cleave did not give Jones an excused absence when Jones missed class for his grandmother’s funeral. Mary will have to miss class to attend her aunt’s funeral. Therefore, Dr. Van Cleave should not give Mary an excused absence either.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a story as strong or not strong. the first person did not give the second person a reason not to go to school when the second person did not go to school for english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Dr. Van Cleave did not give Jones an excused absence when Jones missed class for his brother’s birthday party. Mary will have to miss class to attend her aunt’s funeral. Therefore, Dr. Van Cleave should not give Mary an excused absence either. 159
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a story as strong or not strong. the first person did not give the second person a reason not to go to school when the second person did not go to school for his brother big day party. the third person will have to not go to school to go to her aunt big day party. so the first person should not give the third person a reason not to go to school either.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

If health insurance companies pay for heart surgery and brain surgery, which can both increase an individual’s happiness, then they should also pay for cosmetic surgery, which can also increase an individual’s happiness.
ugf_translation: look at the talk that looks like a story as strong or not strong. if people who give money to help sick people pay for heart cut and brain cut, which can both make a person feel better, then they should also pay for body cut, which can also make a person feel better.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

A knife is an eating utensil that can cut things. A spoon is also an eating utensil. So a spoon can probably cut things as well.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a thing as strong or not strong. a thing that cuts is a thing that can cut things. a thing that catches food is also a thing that can cut things. so a thing that catches food can probably cut things as well.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

Any artificial, complex object like a watch or a telescope has been designed by some intelligent human designer. But naturally occurring objects like eyes and brains are also very complex objects. Therefore, complex naturally occurring objects must have been designed by some intelligent non-human designer.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a picture as strong or not strong. any made up hard thing like a watch or a thing that looks at the sky has been made by some good at thinking person who makes things. but things that happen by itself like eyes and brains are also very hard things. so hard things that happen by itself must have been made by some good at thinking person who makes things.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

The world record holding runner, Kenenisa Bekele ran 100 miles per week and twice a week did workouts comprised of ten mile repeats on the track in the weeks leading up to his 10,000 meter world record. I have run 100 miles per week and have been doing ten mile repeats twice a week. Therefore, the next race I will run will probably be a world record.
ugf_translation: look at the talk about a story as strong or not strong. the world thing that holds a person who runs, the person we call a name, ran a hundred long runs each week and two weeks did work out of ten long runs on the long run in the weeks that lead to his ten long runs world thing. i have run a hundred long runs each week and have been doing ten long runs twice a week. so the next run i will run will probably be a world thing.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

I feel pain when someone hits me in the face with a hockey puck. We are both human beings, so you also probably feel pain when you are hit in the face with a hockey puck.
ugf_translation: look at the kind of talk as strong or not strong. i feel hurt when someone hits me in the face with a ball game. we are both people, so you also probably feel hurt when you hit me in the face with a ball game.

english_source:
Evaluate the analogical argument as strong or weak.

The color I experience when I see something as “green” has a particular quality (that is difficult to describe). You and I are both human beings, so the color you experience when you see something green probably has the exact same quality. (That is, what you and I experience when we see something green is the exact same experiential color.)
ugf_translation: look at the kind of talk as strong or not strong. the color i feel when i see a thing as green has a certain kind of thing that is hard to talk about. you and i are both people, so the color you feel when i see a thing green probably has the same kind of thing. that is, what you and i feel when we see a thing green is the same kind of thing.

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a positive correlation between U.S. spending on science, space, and technology (A) and suicides by hanging, strangulation, and suffocation (B).
ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find out if the first thing makes the second thing happen the second thing makes the first thing happen a same thing that makes both is happening or the thing that goes together is by a thing that is not planned there is a good thing that goes together between the country spending on study of how things work in space and new things and people who kill by hanging and making a bad move and a bad move

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a positive correlation between our dog Charlie’s weight (A) and the amount of time we spend away from home (B). That is, the more time we spend away from home, the heavier Charlie gets (and the more we are at home, the lighter Charlie is.
ugf_translation: for each pair find if the first thing makes the second thing happen, the second thing makes the first thing happen, a same thing that makes both is happening, or the pair is just by chance. there is a good pair between the weight of our dog the first thing and the time we spend away from home. that is, the more time we spend away from home, the heavier the dog gets (and the more we are at home, the lighter the dog is)

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

The height of the tree in our front yard (A) positively correlates with the height of the shrub in our backyard (B).
ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find out if the first thing makes the second thing happen the second thing makes the first thing happen a same thing that makes both is happening or the thing that goes together is just by chance the size of the tree in our front area the first thing goes up in a good way with the size of the tree in our outside area the second thing

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a negative correlation between the number of suicide bombings in the U.S. (A) and the number of hairs on a particular U.S President’s head (B).
ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find if the first thing makes the second thing happen the second thing makes the first thing happen a same thing that makes both is happening or the thing that goes together is just by chance there is a bad thing that goes together between the number of kills in the big country and the number of hair on a head of the big country the second thing

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a high positive correlation between the number of fire engines in a particular borough of New York City (A) and the number of fires that occur there (B). ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find out if the first thing makes the second thing happen, the second thing makes the first thing happen, a same thing that makes both is happening, or the thing that goes together is just by chance. there is a big good go together between the number of fire things in a certain city and the number of fire things that happen there. case two not the first thing or the second thing case three not the second thing case four not the third thing case four not the third thing case one the second thing or the third thing case four not the third thing case two not the first thing or the second thing case three not the second thing or the third thing case four not the third thing case one the first thing the second thing the third thing case four the second thing case two not the first thing or the second thing case three not the second thing or the third thing case four not the third thing case one the first thing the second thing the third thing case four the second thing case two not the first thing or the second thing case three not the second thing or the third thing case four not the third thing case one not the first thing the second thing the third thing case four the third thing case one not the first thing the second thing the third thing case four not the third thing case two not the first thing the third thing case four not the third thing case two not the first thing the third thing case four not the third thing case two not the first thing the third thing case four not the third thing case three not the first thing the second thing the third thing case four not the third thing case four not the third thing case one not the first thing the second thing the third thing case four not the third thing case four

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

At one point in history, there was a negative correlation between the number of mules in the state (A) and the salaries paid to professors at the state university (B). That is, the more mules, the lower the professors’ salaries.
ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find if the first thing makes the second thing happen the second thing makes the first thing happen a same thing that makes both is happening or the thing that goes together is by a thing that is not meant at one time in the past there was a bad thing that goes together between the number of small people in the state and the pay paid to teachers at the state school that is the second thing that is the more small people the less pay for teachers

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

There is a strong positive correlation between the number of traffic accidents on a particular highway (A) and the number of billboards featuring scantily-clad models (B).
ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find out if the first thing makes the second thing happen the second thing makes the first thing happen a same thing that makes both is happening or the thing that goes together is just by chance there is a strong good thing that goes together between the number of cars that go wrong on a certain road and the number of papers that show pictures that are very clear the second thing

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

The girth of an adult’s waist (A) is negatively correlated with the height of their vertical leap (B).
ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find out if the first thing makes the second thing happen, the second thing makes the first thing happen, a same thing that makes both is happening, or the thing that goes together is by a thing that is not planned the leg of a young person the first thing goes down in a bad way with the top of their jump the second thing

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

Olympic marathon times (A) are positively correlated with the temperature during the marathon (B). That is, the more time it takes an Olympic marathoner to complete the race, the higher the temperature.
ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find if the first thing makes the second thing happen the second thing makes the first thing happen a same thing that makes both is happening or the thing that goes together is just by chance the big run time the first thing goes up together with the heat during the big run time that is the more time it takes a person who runs the big run time to finish the run the more heat

english_source:
For each correlation, identify whether A causes B, B causes A, a common cause is at work, or the correlation is accidental.

The number gray hairs on an individual’s head (A) is positively correlated with the number of children or grandchildren they have (B).
ugf_translation: for each thing that goes together find out if the first thing makes the second thing happen the second thing makes the first thing happen a same thing that makes both is happening or the thing that goes together is just by chance the number of gray hair on a person head goes up together with the number of children or children they have
